title: leontopodium × macranthum
intro: Leontopodium × macranthum, also known as Edelweiss, is a hybrid plant species of the genus Leontopodium, native to the European Alps. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that aid in wound healing and the treatment of various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other respiratory issues. Edelweiss is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in ointments and salves for topical application.
